description_long: This is a roughly oval, flat stone with a dark gray to brown coloration and a slightly irregular, natural shape. The surface is mostly matte and uneven, with visible textural variations and some minor chips along the edges. One side of the stone features a white painted marking, possibly an identification number, which stands out against the darker background. The stone appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or artificial modifications aside from the painted marking. Its thickness is relatively uniform, and the top surface is broad enough to support small objects. The overall appearance suggests it has been weathered but remains structurally intact, suitable for use as a natural barrier or obstacle in an urban environment.
description: A flat, dark gray-brown stone with a rough, matte surface and a white painted marking on one side. Its irregular shape and solid build make it suitable as a natural urban barrier.
description_view_0: The stone is seen from an angle highlighting its broad, flat surface and the prominent white marking on the side.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the stone's thin profile and the continuation of the white marking along the edge.
description_view_2: From this angle, the stone's irregular edges and surface texture are most visible, with less emphasis on the painted marking.
description_view_3: The opposite side shows a smoother, unmarked surface, revealing the stone's natural coloration and subtle weathering.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: No regular pattern; surface shows natural mottling and minor chips, with a painted white marking on one side.
attribute_weathering: Moderately weathered with a rough, matte surface and minor edge chipping, indicating exposure to outdoor elements.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground surfaces or landscaping, but the white marking provides a visual contrast in urban settings.
